senses.definition: plural of recruitor, a person or entity that recruits people or items into a group or organization
examples: The company hired several recruitors to help fill open positions.
Recruitors typically screen candidates before presenting them to managers.

senses.definition: people who are recruited or enlisted, especially new members or employees
examples: The recruits report to training camp next Monday.
Many recruits joined the army after finishing school.

senses.definition: to enlist someone for service or a task; to hire people to join an organization
examples: The company recruits graduates from top universities.
They recruited volunteers for the project.

senses.definition: a nonstandard or rare term related to recruitment; not widely used in contemporary English
examples: The term recruity appeared in old texts referring to recruitment practices.

senses.definition: to crush again or anew; to crush a second time
examples: The team tried to recrush the coffee beans to extract more flavor.

senses.definition: a device or person that crushes again; a second crusher
examples: The machine acts as a recrusher in the processing line.

senses.definition: to form crystals again or anew; to recrystallize
examples: The solution will recrystallise as it cools.
This process allows the compound to recrystallise into purer crystals.

senses.definition: -forming crystals again; having undergone recrystallisation
examples: The recrystallised product was purer than before.
After cooling, the solution became recrystallised.

senses.definition: third person singular of recrystallise; forms crystals again
examples: The solution recrystallises as it cools.
Purity improves as the substance recrystallises.

senses.definition: present participle of recrystallise; in the process of forming crystals again
examples: The compound is recrystallising during purification.
They are recrystallising the mixture to obtain purer crystals.

senses.definition: the process of forming crystals again; a method of purifying solids
examples: Recrystallization is often used to purify a chemical compound.
The recrystallization step removes impurities.

senses.definition: plural of recrystallization; multiple instances of the crystal-forming purification process
examples: Several recrystallizations were performed to ensure purity.
The lab compared different recrystallizations to optimize yield.

senses.definition: to form crystals again; to purify by crystallization
examples: If the solution cools slowly, the compound will recrystallize.
We will recrystallize the product to remove impurities.

senses.definition: formed crystals again; purified by crystallization
examples: The recrystallized product showed higher purity.
They had recrystallized the compound multiple times.

senses.definition: third person singular of recrystallize; forms crystals again
examples: The compound recrystallizes from the solution as it cools.
Different solvents cause the substance to recrystallize differently.

senses.definition: present participle of recrystallize; in the process of crystal formation again
examples: The mixture is recrystallizing slowly.
We are recrystallizing the sample to study impurity patterns.

senses.definition: abbreviation or informal shorthand for records or recs; also short for recommendations in some contexts
examples: The file contains several music recs.
She saved the movie recs to her list.

senses.definition: short for rectangle or rectifier depending on context; abbreviation used in geometry or electronics
examples: Draw a rect on the graph.
The rect in the circuit is used to convert AC to DC.

senses.definition: plural or feminine form in some languages; in English often seen as a fragment or misspelling
examples: The term recta appeared in the document, likely a truncation.

senses.definition: relating to the rectum, the last part of the digestive system
examples: Rectal cancer screening is important for adults.
The doctor noted a rectal examination was needed.

senses.definition: pain in the rectal area
examples: Rectalgia can be a symptom of various digestive conditions.

senses.definition: relating to or performed through the rectum
examples: The medication was administered rectally.
Rectally inserted enema may be used in some treatments.

senses.definition: a four-sided shape with opposite sides parallel and equal in length
examples: Draw a rectangle on the paper.
A rectangle has four right angles.

senses.definition: having the shape of a rectangle; square-angled
examples: Rectangled corners improve stability.

senses.definition: plural of rectangle; more than one four-sided shape with opposite sides parallel and equal
examples: The worksheet shows several rectangles.
Find the area of these rectangles.

senses.definition: having the shape of a rectangle
examples: A rectangular table fits the room well.
The diagram shows a rectangular outline.
